Skip to content Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema criterion entity-ref example extends extensions link presentation rich-text status system-info use-cases blueprint component foundation guide pattern theme token accessibility anatomy api content design-specifications document-blocks guidelines imports interactions motion principles scale sections states steps variants aliases category extends last-updated links metadata preview since status summary tags thumbnail
Root Properties The DSDS spec version this document conforms to. URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation. Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document — usually a core design system. The base document provides the core entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities. It supports systems-of-systems architectures, where product- or brand-specific systems inherit from a shared core. System-level structured documentation for the design system as a whole — its purpose (when to adopt the system), cross-cutting guidelines, and narrative overviews. Each entry is a general document block: a useCases, guidelines, sections, accessibility, or content block. This is the same documentBlocks mechanism entities use, scoped to the system rather than a single artifact. Position signals the scope (root vs. inside an entity). One or more entity groups. Each group is a named collection with a single entities array. That array holds any mix of entity types — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, guides, and blueprints — in display order. Each entity's kind identifies its type. Multiple groups let one DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components). Groups may be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file.
Min items: 1 A single entity document — one component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or blueprint. Use this instead of entityGroups when each entity lives in its own file. The kind field on the entity identifies its type.

3 definitions in this file:

  • entityGroup
  • anyEntity
  • fileRef
 Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This is already a display label — it does NOT have an identifier counterpart and need not match a slug pattern. The entities in this group, in display order. Any mix of entity kinds — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, guides, and blueprints. Each item's kind identifies its type. Items may be inlined or referenced through a $ref object that points to another DSDS file. Order is significant: tools SHOULD keep it for display.
Min items: 1 References: richText, anyEntity, fileRef, extensions

Accepts one of:

  • component
  • pattern
  • foundation
  • theme
  • token
  • tokenGroup
  • guide
  • blueprint
References: component, pattern, foundation, theme, token, tokenGroup, guide, blueprint A relative reference to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment that identifies the target value within that file (e.g., './button.dsds.json#/entity'). MUST be relative. Absolute paths, protocol-relative references, and scheme-bearing URIs (http:, file:, …) are not permitted. A reference to another system uses extends.url. Resolvers MUST detect cycles. They MUST treat unresolvable files or pointers and shape mismatches as fatal. They MUST gate any remote dereferencing behind an allow-list (see the spec's reference-resolution and security rules).

Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) 0.10.2 — Draft Specification

GitHub
